research_context:
## Shape & Geometry
- Nozzle Design - NASA: The exit velocity, pressure, and mass flow through the nozzle determines the amount of thrust produced by the nozzle. On this slide we derive the equations which explain and describe why a supersonic flow accelerates in the divergent section of the nozzle while a subsonic flow decelerates in a diver
- Bell nozzle - Wikipedia: The bell or contour shape is designed to impart a large angle expansion for the gases right after the throat. The nozzle is then curved back in to give a nearly straight flow of gas out the nozzle opening. The contour used is rather complex. The large expansion section near the throat causes expansi
- PDF Rocket Nozzle Geometries - gatech.edu: Major Nozzle Configurations Four major types (based on diverging section) Conical oldest, simplest design, easy to manufacture Bell/contoured complex shape, high efficiency Plug/aerospike altitude compensating, annular or linear Expansion-deflection altitude compensating, shorter than other enclosed
- CFD Analysis of Different Shape Rocket Nozzles - Springer: The two different nozzles which were studied in the present work are (a) the convergent-divergent nozzle and (b) the bell-shaped nozzle. The CFD analysis is carried out using ANSYS 22.0 software to obtain velocity, pressure, temperature, and the flow pattern.
- Performance increment of bell nozzles: Implementation of passive flow ...: The performance differs with each geometry concerning other parameters. During high-speed operation, in Convergent-Divergent (C-D) nozzles, the thickness of boundary layer increases drastically, developing lambda shocks, shock trains and pressure oscillations at the exit leading to imperfect expansi

## Dimensions
- Interactive Nozzle Simulator - NASA: A jet nozzle will have a larger throat area and a lower chamber total pressure than a rocket nozzle. If you select the Geometry input, you can use the choice button to choose either a pure convergent nozzle or a convergent-dive
build_recipe:
STEP 1: Create base plate — cq.Workplane("XY").box(60, 80, 3)
STEP 2: Cut center bore — .faces(">Z").workplane().circle(25/2).cutThruAll()
STEP 3: Add throat section — .workplane(offset=3).circle(24.2294665788126/2).extrude(20.983333577397737)
STEP 4: Add divergent section — .faces(">Z").workplane().circle(120.74525598700315/2).cutThruAll()
STEP 5: Add convergent section — .faces("<Z").workplane(offset=60-24.2294665788126/2).circle(36.344199868218894/2).extrude(20.983333577397737)
STEP 6: Add exit section — .faces("<Z").workplane(offset=60-120.74525598700315/2).circle(60/2).extrude(20.983333577397737)
STEP 7: Smooth the surface — .faces("<Z").fillet(1)
